indexes.name: idx_hyp_lineitem_shipdate_orderkey
indexes.workload_pattern: Filters on l_shipdate in Q1, Q5, Q14, Q15, Q20 and join/filter on l_orderkey in several TPC-H queries; useful for date-driven access with subsequent order lookup.
indexes.benefits: Improves selectivity for date predicates and can help combine range filtering with join navigation on lineitem.
indexes.tradeoffs: Extra storage and slower inserts/updates on the largest table; may be less useful when queries only filter by l_shipdate alone.

indexes.name: idx_hyp_lineitem_orderkey_shipdate
indexes.workload_pattern: Join on l_orderkey is central in Q3, Q4, Q6, Q7, Q8, Q9, Q11, Q12, Q17, Q18, Q19, Q20, Q22; l_shipdate also appears in range filters and join-related subqueries.
indexes.benefits: Helps merge lineitem joins by orderkey with shipdate-restricted workloads and can reduce scanned rows for order-centric queries.
indexes.tradeoffs: Competes with a shipdate-first index; column order matters, so it is not a universal fix for all lineitem access paths.

indexes.name: idx_hyp_orders_custkey_orderdate
indexes.workload_pattern: Frequent joins on o_custkey plus orderdate ranges in Q3, Q4, Q7, Q9, Q10, Q11, Q12, Q17, Q18, Q22.
indexes.benefits: Speeds customer-to-orders lookups while preserving range filtering on order date for time-window queries.
indexes.tradeoffs: Adds maintenance cost on orders; if queries mostly filter by date only, the existing single-column date index may be preferable.

indexes.name: idx_hyp_customer_mktsegment_custkey
indexes.workload_pattern: Q3 filters by c_mktsegment and joins via c_custkey to orders.
indexes.benefits: Supports selective segment filtering followed by fast join into orders, reducing the customer set early.
indexes.tradeoffs: Limited benefit beyond segment-driven queries; extra index overhead on a relatively small table.

indexes.name: idx_hyp_part_type_size
indexes.workload_pattern: Q2 filters on p_type and p_size; Q8/Q15/Q16 also filter on part attributes, with p_type and p_size appearing together in key predicates.
indexes.benefits: Improves combined filtering on part classification attributes and can reduce candidate rows before joins to partsupp/lineitem.
indexes.tradeoffs: May not help when queries use only one of the two columns; existing single-column indexes already cover each separately.

indexes.name: idx_hyp_part_brand_container
indexes.workload_pattern: Q19 uses p_brand with p_container lists in multiple OR branches; Q15/Q16/Q18 also rely on brand-related filters.
indexes.benefits: Speeds combined brand/container predicates and helps prune part rows earlier in branch-heavy queries.
indexes.tradeoffs: OR-heavy predicates may still limit index efficiency; maintenance overhead on part updates.

indexes.name: idx_hyp_supplier_nationkey_suppkey
indexes.workload_pattern: Used in joins from supplier to nation and lineitem in Q4, Q5, Q7, Q8, Q9, Q10, Q11, Q16, Q20.
indexes.benefits: Supports nation-based supplier filtering with quick supplier key access for downstream joins.
indexes.tradeoffs: Less helpful for workloads that join only on s_suppkey or only filter by nationkey; adds write cost.

indexes.name: idx_hyp_partsupp_partkey_suppkey
indexes.workload_pattern: Core join path in Q2, Q8, Q9, Q10, Q15, Q20 where partsupp is matched by part and supplier keys.
indexes.benefits: Accelerates the dominant join pattern on partsupp and can reduce lookup cost in correlated subqueries.
indexes.tradeoffs: Additional storage on an 8000-row table; benefit is concentrated in join-heavy queries.

indexes.name: idx_hyp_lineitem_shipdate_recent
indexes.workload_pattern: Many workload queries filter on shipdate windows in the mid-to-late 1990s, especially Q1, Q5, Q14, Q15, Q20.
indexes.benefits: Shrinks the index to the more frequently queried recent rows, improving cache residency and range scan speed for date-window queries.
indexes.tradeoffs: Only helps queries inside the chosen date range; not useful for older or broad shipdate scans.

indexes.name: idx_hyp_orders_recent_orderdate
indexes.workload_pattern: Q3, Q4, Q7, Q9, Q10, Q11, Q12, Q17, Q18, Q22 use orderdate windows concentrated in the 1993-1998 range.
indexes.benefits: Creates a smaller, more selective index for the active portion of the orders timeline used by the workload.
indexes.tradeoffs: Less general than a full index; queries outside the filtered range will not benefit.

indexes.name: idx_hyp_lineitem_returnflag_receiptdate
indexes.workload_pattern: Q10 filters on returnflag = 'R' with an orderdate window, and Q12 filters on receiptdate ranges with shipping constraints.
indexes.benefits: Targets the return-flagged subset and can speed the revenue-by-returned-orders query pattern.
indexes.tradeoffs: Highly specialized; if the data distribution changes or queries shift, the partial predicate may become too narrow.
